events.text: Patient is a 60-year-old White female with a BMI of 25.8. Medical history includes well-controlled hypertension. Maternal aunt had breast cancer.

At age 58, patient detected a right breast mass during self-examination. Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), right breast upper outer quadrant (C50.4), grade 3, ER negative, PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 65%.
At age 58, right breast core biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3 (tubule 3, nuclear 3, mitotic 3). ER negative (
At age 58, diagnostic mammogram showed a 4.2 cm mass with irregular margins in the right upper outer quadrant with ipsilateral axillary adenopathy, BIRADS 5.
At age 58, staging PET/CT showed right breast mass (SUVmax 14.2), right axillary nodes (SUVmax 10.1), and no distant metastatic disease.
At age 58, stage IIIA (cT2N2M0) right breast invasive ductal carcinoma, HER2-positive, ER/PR-negative. Locally advanced, non-metastatic.
At age 58, received neoadjuvant TCHP (docetaxel, carboplatin, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) for 6 cycles.

At age 58, right mastectomy and axillary dissection post-neoadjuvant: residual 2.1 cm invasive ductal carcinoma with partial treatment response. 3 of 16 axillary nodes positive (largest deposit 5 mm). ypT2 ypN1a. RCB class II.
At age 58, underwent right modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection. Residual disease present after neoadjuvant therapy.
At age 59, given residual disease, received adjuvant T-DM1 for 14 cycles per KATHERINE protocol.
At age 59, adjuvant radiation to the right chest wall and regional nodes, 50 Gy in 25 fractions with 10 Gy boost to mastectomy scar.
At age 58, during neoadjuvant TCHP: grade 3 neutropenia (received pegfilgrastim), grade 2 nausea, grade 2 alopecia, grade 2 diarrhea. LVEF 58% stable.
At age 59, during adjuvant T-DM1: grade 2 thrombocytopenia (nadir 65,000, dose reduced to 3.0 mg/kg), grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 elevated AST. LVEF 55%.
At age 59, surveillance CT after completing T-DM1 showed no evidence of recurrent disease. Post-mastectomy changes only.
At age 59, completed adjuvant T-DM1 and radiation. No endocrine therapy indicated (ER/PR-negative). Surveillance: exam every 3-4 months, annual imaging.
At age 60, patient developed severe headaches and visual disturbance. MRI brain showed 5 enhancing brain metastases (bilateral cerebral hemispheres and cerebellum, largest 2.5 cm right parietal).
At age 60, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed no extracranial disease recurrence. Brain-only recurrence.
At age 60, recurrent HER2-positive breast cancer with isolated brain metastases. CNS-only disease recurrence 18 months after completing adjuvant therapy.
At age 60, underwent whole brain radiation therapy (WBRT) 30 Gy in 10 fractions with hippocampal avoidance technique, plus SRS boost to the 2 largest lesions (18 Gy single fraction each).
At age 60, initiated trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd, Enhertu) 5.4 mg/kg every 21 days given CNS activity data and brain metastases.
At age 60, on T-DXd: grade 2 nausea (managed with ondansetron and dexamethasone), grade 1 fatigue, grade 1 alopecia. Monitored for interstitial lung disease with regular CT assessment.
At age 60, MRI brain 3 months after WBRT and T-DXd showed significant reduction in brain metastases. 3 of 5 lesions resolved. Partial intracranial response.
At age 60, good response to T-DXd with brain metastasis improvement. ECOG 1. Tolerating therapy. No signs of ILD on CT. Continue T-DXd. Pulmonary monitoring ongoing.
At age 58, tumor genomic profiling on surgical specimen: ERBB2 amplification (copy number 14), TP53 G245D, RB1 loss, CDK12 rearrangement. TMB 7 mutations/Mb. MSS.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
